sections.title: A: Error correction
sections.instructions: Each of the numbered words or phrases in bold is incorrect. Rewrite them correctly.

items.sentence: Last month, I wrote to a few local television **station** asking if they gave away **the** free tickets to any shows.
A week later, they sent **to me** two tickets for a quiz show.
I went with my best friend Angie last night. It was **an** excellent!
The show is called Know Them Or Not? and contestants have to answer questions about **a lots** of different people in their lives, like friends, relatives and colleagues.
There are ten contestants to start with but one of them gets knocked out at the end of each round.
I'd like to go into **on** a show like that one day but, for now, I'm **much** happy just to have been in the audience.

sections.title: B: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals
sections.instructions: Complete the sentences by changing the form of the word in capitals when this is necessary.

items.sentence: Why can't (POLITICS) ______ ever just say 'yes' or 'no' when they're asked if they agree with something?
I was hoping this book would help me with my history project but in fact it wasn't very (INFORM) ______.
My cousin Dan has kept a (JOURNAL) ______ since he was twelve years old.
Did you hear that (ANNOUNCE) ______ on the radio about the water shortage?
There's an (WRITE) ______ rule in our house about how late we can stay up to watch TV.
I just stood there in (BELIEVE) ______ when I heard I'd been selected to go on the quiz show.
The Internet is an incredibly powerful means of (COMMUNICATE) ______.
I wouldn't describe the film as a comedy, but one or two scenes are mildly (HUMOUR) ______.

sections.title: C: Complete the second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
These two dictionaries are similar.
little
There ______ these two dictionaries.

My first editor greatly influenced me.
influence
My first editor ______ me.

They described the robber on the evening news.
gave
They ______ the robber on the evening news.

Robert Maxdoch has just bought the newspaper.
control
The newspaper is now ______ Robert Maxdoch.

You'll be wasting your time if you try to get tickets for that show.
point
There ______ to get tickets for that show.

I believe that adverts do influence people's buying habits.
view
It ______ that adverts do influence people's buying habits.

The prime minister will probably make a statement this afternoon.
likely
The prime minister ______ a statement this afternoon.

A programme about the royal family will be shown instead of Friends this evening.
place
This evening, Friends, ______ a programme about the royal family will be shown.

The Hollywood star wouldn't say anything about the allegations.
comment
The Hollywood star refused ______ the allegations.
How ______ information do we have at the moment?
Only a ______ print journalists manage to move into television.
This magazine's got ______ of adverts in it.
I won ______ money on a quiz show once.
There's ______ chance of getting rich from starting a website.
I think that ______ children's TV programmes these days are educational.
There aren't ______ opportunities for poets to get published.
The programme usually ______ on at half past eight.
I saw the interview while I was ______ through a magazine at the hairdresser's.
Why did you have to bring that subject ______ at dinner?
They mentioned it in the article but they didn't ______ into detail.
The tabloids completely ______ that story about Bruce Willis. It's not true at all!
They were ______ out free tickets to the concert to everyone in the shopping centre.
We had the volume turned down, so I couldn't make ______ what they were talking about.
